heading: The National Alzheimer’s Project
label: 11225 The National Alzheimer’s Project
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsecs. (a), (b), (e)(1), and (h), is , , , known as the National Alzheimer’s Project Act, which enacted this subchapter and provisions set out as a note under . For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 2010 Amendment note set out under and Tables.Pub. CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Alzheimer’s Project Act, and not as part of the Alzheimer’s Disease and Related Dementias Research Act of 1992 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Definition of Alzheimer’s
children.label: a Definition of Alzheimer’s
children.text: In this Act, the term “Alzheimer’s” means Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Office of the Secretary of Health and Human Services the National Alzheimer’s Project (referred to in this Act as the “Project”).
be responsible for the creation and maintenance of an integrated national plan to overcome Alzheimer’s;
provide information on, and coordination of, Alzheimer’s research and services across all Federal agencies;
accelerate the development of treatments that would prevent, halt, or reverse the course of Alzheimer’s;

children.label: A
children.text: promotion of healthy aging and reduction and mitigation of risk factors for Alzheimer’s;

children.label: B
children.text: early diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease; and

children.label: C
children.text: coordination of the care and treatment of citizens with Alzheimer’s;

ensure the inclusion of ethnic and racial populations and other underserved populations, including individuals with developmental disabilities such as Down syndrome, at higher risk for Alzheimer’s or least likely to receive care, in clinical, research, and service efforts with the purpose of decreasing health disparities in Alzheimer’s;
provide information on, and promote the adoption of, healthy behaviors that may reduce the risk of cognitive decline and promote and protect cognitive health; and
coordinate with international bodies to integrate and inform the fight against Alzheimer’s globally.

children.label: A
children.text: oversee the creation and updating of the national plan described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: use discretionary authority to evaluate all Federal programs around Alzheimer’s, including budget requests and approvals.

children.heading: National plan
children.label: 2 National plan
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services, or the Secretary’s designee, shall carry out an annual assessment of the Nation’s progress, across public and private sectors, in preparing for the escalating burden of Alzheimer’s, including both implementation steps and recommendations for priority actions based on the assessment, including consideration of public-private collaborations, as appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established an Advisory Council on Alzheimer’s Research, Care, and Services (referred to in this Act as the “Advisory Council”).

children.label: i
children.text: A designee of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

children.label: ii
children.text: A designee of the Administration on Aging.

children.label: iii
children.text: A designee of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.label: iv
children.text: A designee of the Indian Health Service.

children.label: v
children.text: A designee of the Office of the Director of the National Institutes of Health.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Surgeon General.

children.label: vii
children.text: A designee of the National Science Foundation.

children.label: viii
children.text: A designee of the Department of Veterans Affairs.

children.label: ix
children.text: A designee of the Food and Drug Administration.

children.label: x
children.text: A designee of the Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality.

children.label: xi
children.text: A designee of the Department of Justice.

children.label: xii
children.text: A designee of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.label: xiii
children.text: A designee of the Social Security Administration.

children.label: xiv
children.text: 2 or more other designees, as determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, at least one of whom has expertise in risk factors associated with the development or the progression of Alzheimer’s.

children.label: i
children.text: 2 Alzheimer’s patient advocates;

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 Alzheimer’s caregivers;

children.label: iii
children.text: 2 health care providers;

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 representatives of State health departments;

children.label: v
children.text: 3 researchers with Alzheimer’s-related expertise in basic, translational, clinical, or drug development science, including at least one researcher with demonstrated experience in recruitment and retention of underrepresented groups into research or clinical trials related to dementia;

children.label: vi
children.text: 2 voluntary health association representatives, including a national Alzheimer’s disease organization that has demonstrated experience in research, care, and patient services, and a State-based advocacy organization that provides services to families and professionals, including information and referral, support groups, care consultation, education, and safety services;

children.label: vii
children.text: 1 individual with a diagnosis of Alzheimer’s disease; and

children.label: viii
children.text: 1 representative from a historically underserved population whose lifetime risk for developing Alzheimer’s is markedly higher than that of other populations.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 3 Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Council shall meet quarterly and such meetings shall be open to the public.

children.heading: Advice
children.label: 4 Advice
children.text: The Advisory Council shall advise the Secretary of Health and Human Services, or the Secretary’s designee.

children.label: A
children.text: annual evaluations of all federally funded efforts in Alzheimer’s research, risk reduction, public health, clinical care, and institutional-, home-, and community-based programs and their outcomes;

children.label: B
children.text: recommendations for priority actions to expand, eliminate, coordinate, or condense programs based on the program’s performance, mission, and purpose;

children.label: I
children.text: Medicare and other federally funded programs; and

children.label: II
children.text: families living with Alzheimer’s disease; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improve health outcomes and reduce disparities; and

children.label: D
children.text: annual evaluations of the implementation, including outcomes, of the recommendations, including priorities if necessary, through an updated national plan under subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Termination
children.label: 6 Termination
children.text: The Advisory Council shall terminate on .December 31, 2035

children.heading: Data sharing
children.label: f Data sharing
children.text: Agencies both within the Department of Health and Human Services and outside of the Department that have data relating to Alzheimer’s shall share such data with the Secretary of Health and Human Services, or the Secretary’s designee, to enable the Secretary, or the Secretary’s designee, to complete the report described in subsection (g).

children.label: A
children.text: an evaluation; of all federally funded efforts in Alzheimer’s research, clinical care, and institutional-, home-, and community-based programs and their outcomes; and1So in original. The semicolon probably should not appear.1

children.label: B
children.text: a summary of the Secretary’s process for identifying and updating what conditions constitute Alzheimer’s disease;
an evaluation of all federally funded programs based on program performance, mission, and purpose related to Alzheimer’s disease;

children.label: I
children.text: Medicare and other federally funded programs; and

children.label: II
children.text: families living with Alzheimer’s disease; and

children.label: ii
children.text: improve health outcomes and reduce disparities;

children.label: B
children.text: implementation steps; and

children.label: C
children.text: priority actions to improve the prevention, diagnosis, treatment, care, institutional-, home-, and community-based programs of Alzheimer’s disease for individuals with Alzheimer’s disease and their caregivers; and
an annually updated national plan.

children.heading: Professional judgment budget
children.label: h Professional judgment budget
children.text: For fiscal year 2024 and each subsequent fiscal year, the Director of the National Institutes of Health shall prepare and submit, directly to the President for review and transmittal to Congress, after reasonable opportunity for comment, but without change, by the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the Advisory Council, an annual budget estimate for the initiatives of the National Institutes of Health pursuant to the reports and recommendations made under this Act, including an estimate of the number and type of personnel needs for the National Institutes of Health.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: i Sunset
children.text: The Project shall expire on .December 31, 2035
